name: xf_cep_not_bf_arrival2
drl: rule NE when not E0() P() then end
comment: D-146/D-149 FENCED (order-only, deterministic): BLOCKER-FIRST `not E0() P()` with mid-run E0 arrivals — unvalidated by D-140 (no arrivals in its population). Engine (d140 `not_order_key`; the seg branch never engages, no P has ins_seg==0) ~31% divergent (population: SEINE_NOTPOP_BF_ONLY=1 tools/fuzz_notorder_b.py, seeds 861-863, 694 cases). D-149 RECON: the INSERT/ARRIVAL structure is EXACTLY cracked — per-epoch ins-batches, newest-epoch first, within-batch (ins_seg DESC, gidx); ZERO update-free mismatches. What resists is UPDATE placement: a 3456-combo parametric sweep ($JOB/tmp/bfsim.py — promotion classes {epoch-0|ep>=1}x{same|cross-seg}x{mid|preADV|postADV}, epoch-join vs pool, pool keys, max-touch bumping, front/tail/adjacent final-join) caps at 72.2%. Smoking-gun flip-flop pairs (probe batteries $JOB/tmp/bfprobe.py + inline): bf_P1_pre [1,4,3,2] (single epoch-0 cross final-pre promotion FRONTS) vs nb861x58 [2,1] (same class TAILS); d_ctl [2,3,1,4] fronts vs x110-shape tails vs d_P4P1 [1,4,3,2] fronts — no static adjacency/touch/count rule survives; apply-ORDER sensitivity confirmed (d_addP4first/last). CONCLUSION: the same signature as pre-D-125 temporal joins — the order falls out of the per-arrival STREAM flush/staging machinery (join-emission prepends, per-cycle windows), NOT a per-fact sort key. To close: the GRAFT + flush-simulation methodology (D-125/D-138 — a staging dump on the not->join path + a faithful per-arrival simulator like model_join_flush.py), then port. SET 0-div; out of every validated distribution + gen.rs. See DECISIONS D-149.
